channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Large crowds gather in Birmingham for Europe’s largest Eid al-Fitr celebration
item.description: Thousands of people gathered at Small Heath Park in Birmingham to take part in what is considered the largest Eid al-Fitr celebration in Europe. The event drew attendees from across the Midlands, with...

item.title: Why child-free couples are choosing dogs over children in modern Britain
item.description: Across the United Kingdom, a growing number of couples are choosing dogs instead of children, creating a social trend captured by the term 'Dinkwads' (dual income, no kids, with a dog). With fertility...

item.title: BARMM voter registration hits 84,859 as Comelec readies for first parliamentary elections
item.description: As of March 19, voter registration in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) has reached 84,859, signaling progress in the preparations for the region’s first parliamentary electi...

item.title: Buratai Say Naming Terror Financiers Na Government Work, Not Army Matter
item.description: Former Chief of Army Staff, Lieutenant General Tukur Buratai (retd.), don talk say people no suppose blame am for why government never release the names of those wey dey sponsor terrorism for Nigeria....

item.title: Understanding 'Mubarak': Word of the Day, March 21, 2026
item.description: ABS-CBN Lifestyle presents the 'Word of the Day' for March 21, 2026: 'mubarak.' This feature aims to enrich readers' vocabulary by introducing significant words from different languages and cultures. ...

item.title: Chuck Norris, legendary martial artist and actor, passes away at 86
item.description: Chuck Norris, the legendary American martial artist, actor and cultural icon, has passed away at the age of 86. His family confirmed the news on his Instagram account, stating that he died peacefully ...

item.title: Ranchi resident dies on a ship near Strait of Hormuz, family requests help for repatriation
item.description: A man from Ranchi tragically passed away while working on a ship near the Strait of Hormuz amidst rising tensions between Israel and Iran. His family is appealing for assistance to bring his body back...

item.title: Kenyan leaders call for peace and unity during nationwide Idd celebrations
item.description: Leaders and security officials in Kenya have urged Muslims to remain calm and avoid violence as they gather in mosques, open grounds and prayer sites across the country to mark Idd. The call for restr...

item.title: Luc Longley hails Matthew Dellavedova’s leadership and intensity in Sydney Kings’ title push
item.description: Luc Longley, the Australian basketball legend and former Chicago Bull, has praised Matthew Dellavedova’s unmatched intensity and leadership as key to the Sydney Kings’ resurgence in the NBL. When the ...

item.title: South Africa reports reduced road crashes and fatalities ahead of Easter travel period
item.description: Government has reported encouraging progress in reducing road accidents in South Africa, with preliminary figures from 1 January to 15 March 2026 showing an 11% decline in crashes compared to the same...

item.title: Malacañang urges public transport drivers to trust government assistance programs amid fuel crisis
item.description: Malacañang appealed to public utility vehicle (PUV) drivers and operators to trust the government’s actions in addressing the continuous increase in fuel prices. Presidential Communications Undersecre...

item.title: Dr. Gordon Edwards Criticizes Overly Optimistic Claims About Nuclear Energy
item.description: The article 'Debunking Nuclear “Hopium” – Dr. Gordon Edwards,' published on the website Nuclear News, examines the skepticism of Dr. Gordon Edwards toward the overly optimistic narratives surrounding ...

item.title: Providus Bank Surpasses CBN Recapitalisation, Unity Bank Acquisition on Track
item.description: Ahahn! So, Providus Bank, you know, the bank wey dey try buy over Unity Bank, don show CBN say dem don meet the new capital requirement dem give banks. Dem don show say dem capital base na N65 billion...

item.title: Sakaja Leads Citywide Clean-Up to Restore Nairobi’s Drainage Systems After Heavy Rains
item.description: On March 20, 2026, the Nairobi County Government, under Governor Johnson Sakaja, kicked off a citywide operation to clear blocked drainage systems and reduce the risk of flooding caused by continuous ...

item.title: UK Allows US Use of Its Military Bases for Defensive Strikes on Iranian Missile Sites
item.description: Naija people, gist don dey hot for Middle East matter. The British government don allow US to use UK military bases to hit Iranian missile sites wey dey attack ships for Strait of Hormuz. Dis move com...

item.title: Brain mechanisms linking chronic pain and depression revealed by hippocampal changes
item.description: Chronic pain has long been associated with depression, affecting around 40% of sufferers, yet the reasons why only some individuals develop depressive symptoms have remained unclear. A recent study ba...

item.title: Kenyan Military Doctor Gladys Kipsang Celebrates 13 Years of Dedicated Service in the US Army
item.description: Kenyan-born orthopaedic surgeon and US Army officer, Captain Gladys Kipsang, has celebrated an impressive milestone of 13 years serving in the United States military. Kipsang, who hails from Kenya and...

item.title: Asu Beks Urges Presidency to Hand Over NIMASA’s Idle Floating Dockyard to Private Operator
item.description: Elder Asu Beks, wey be CEO of Maritime Media Limited and convener of the Maritime Industry Merit Awards, don call President Bola Ahmed Tinubu make government concession the abandoned floating dockyard...

item.title: SoftBank Announces $710 Billion Ohio Data Centre Campus
item.description: Japanese investment giant SoftBank Group is embarking on a monumental infrastructure project in Ohio, representing a staggering $US500 billion investment, which translates to approximately $710 billio...

item.title: MWO advises OFWs to be cautious of fraudulent repatriation offers
item.description: The Migrant Workers Office (MWO) in Dubai and the Northern Emirates has issued a warning to overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) regarding scams related to repatriation assistance. Fraudsters have reporte...
